# Solutions Index
*Generated — do not edit. Sources: the problems tables and each solutions/ file. Regenerate: `go -C tools/solutions-index run .`*

One row per first-party solution (unpromoted marketplace plugins live in `marketplace.md`). Setup signals are re-checkable disk evidence; session signals are conversation evidence accumulated in the profile. `—` means no signal of that tier exists.

| Id | Kind | Goals | Best when | Setup signal | Session signal |
|----|------|-------|-----------|--------------|----------------|
| agent-sdk | doc | building-agents, llm-features | someone is building an autonomous AI product or teammate, not just using Claude Code interactively | — | the repo imports @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk or discusses building an agent product |
| agent-sdk-dev | plugin | building-agents | starting an Agent SDK project and wanting a validated scaffold over a blank directory | — | agent-sdk-dev is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| autonomous-loops | technique | accessibility, debugging, greenfield, llm-features, migration, performance, refactoring, testing | coverage must reach a specific threshold to merge | — | `/loop` or `/goal` in the transcript; goal-conditioned prompts |
| background-agents | technique | tech-debt | audit takes an hour but your attention shouldn't | — | `claude agents`, `claude --bg`, or `/background` usage; talks about dispatching tasks |
| browser-integration | technique | accessibility, research | verifying tab order, focus traps, or screen reader behavior | — | `claude --chrome` or `/chrome` usage; asks Claude to drive a browser |
| built-in-review-skills | technique | api-design, code-review, security, tech-debt | quick review of a focused diff or your own pre-PR code | Review commands wired into CI workflows | `/code-review`, `/security-review`, `/simplify`, `/verify`, or `/run` in the transcript |
| channels | technique | incident-response | alerts should land in the session with context already loaded | A channel plugin installed (telegram, discord, imessage, fakechat) | `--channels` flag mentions; talks about pushing webhooks/chat into a session |
| checkpoints-rewind | technique | refactoring | risky refactor you might need to undo | — | `/rewind` usage; "undo that" / restore-checkpoint interactions |
| claude-code-action | integration | ci-automation, code-review | a repo wants Claude reviews or automation on every PR without hand-rolling workflow YAML | — | a workflow under .github/workflows/ uses anthropics/claude-code-action |
| claude-code-setup | plugin | onboarding | a repo is new to Claude Code and wants tailored automation recommendations from its actual contents | — | claude-code-setup is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| claude-md-management | plugin | documentation | a CLAUDE.md exists but nobody knows if it is still good — audit it against the real codebase | — | claude-md-management is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| cloud-sessions | technique | code-review | deep review off your machine, or PRs that fix themselves | — | `claude --cloud` (or the deprecated `--remote`), `/teleport`, claude.ai/code or mobile-app mentions |
| code-modernization | plugin | migration | a legacy codebase (COBOL, old Java/C++, monolith) needs a structured migration, not one-shot edits | — | code-modernization is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| code-review | builtin-command | code-review, tech-debt | a diff or PR needs a structured correctness pass before humans read it | — | user ran /code-review or /review in this conversation |
| commit-commands | plugin | release-management | a team wants shared commit conventions and branch hygiene as slash commands | — | commit-commands is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| custom-agents | technique | building-agents, ci-automation, tech-debt | want a working prototype today | `.claude/agents/*.md` or `~/.claude/agents/*.md` exists | References their own named agents |
| custom-plugins | technique | building-skills-plugins | skill works; time to share or distribute | `.claude-plugin/` in a repo they own; a marketplace file they maintain | Talks about publishing or packaging a plugin |
| custom-skills | technique | building-skills-plugins, documentation, greenfield, onboarding, release-management | a workflow you repeat and want to package | `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` or `~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` exists (their own, not plugin-installed) | Invokes their own slash commands |
| deep-research | technique | accessibility, api-design, code-understanding, dependency-management, devops, documentation, greenfield, incident-response, llm-features, onboarding, performance, research, security | evaluating a library you haven't used before | — | `/deep-research` in the transcript |
| fan-out-workflows | technique | ci-automation, code-review, testing | multi-step pipeline with verification between stages | `.claude/workflows/` exists | Workflow/ultracode usage; asks for parallel multi-agent runs |
| feature-dev | plugin | greenfield | a feature deserves a guided explore-design-review pipeline rather than ad-hoc prompting | — | feature-dev is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| frontend-design | plugin | greenfield | UI work should come out production-grade and visually bold by default | — | frontend-design is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| gitlab-ci-integration | integration | ci-automation | the team is on GitLab and wants Claude in the pipeline without GitHub-specific tooling | — | the repo has a .gitlab-ci.yml |
| goal | builtin-command | debugging, migration, testing | the finish line is machine-verifiable (tests green, zero type errors) and human supervision of each iteration adds nothing | — | user ran /goal in this conversation |
| headless-mode | technique | building-mcp-integrations, ci-automation, release-management | running Claude in GitHub Actions or GitLab CI | `claude -p` in `.github/workflows/`, `.gitlab-ci.yml`, or scripts | Discusses non-interactive/CI invocations |
| hookify | plugin | building-skills-plugins, ci-automation | a conversation pattern ("run lint after every edit") should become a real hook without hand-writing settings JSON | — | hookify is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| hooks-as-workflow | technique | accessibility, building-skills-plugins, debugging, performance, security, testing | the behavior should be automatic, not invoked | `hooks` configured in `.claude/settings.json`, `.claude/settings.local.json`, or `~/.claude/settings.json` | Asks about automating actions on edits/commits |
| init | builtin-command | code-understanding, documentation, onboarding | a repo has no CLAUDE.md and every session re-explains the same build commands and conventions | — | a CLAUDE.md generated by /init exists in the repo |
| loop | builtin-command | ci-automation, incident-response | work should recur on a time interval — polling a deploy, re-checking a queue — rather than converge on a condition | — | user ran /loop in this conversation |
| lsp-self-correction | technique | code-understanding | tracing how components connect across a large codebase | An LSP plugin installed for the project language | Mentions go-to-definition / diagnostics-driven fixes |
| mcp-context | technique | api-design, building-mcp-integrations, code-review, code-understanding, dependency-management, documentation, incident-response, onboarding | existing docs, specs, and decisions are scattered | `.mcp.json` exists, or MCP servers configured in settings | Uses MCP-backed tools; mentions connecting external systems |
| mcp-server-dev | plugin | building-mcp-integrations | writing an MCP server and wanting current SDK idioms instead of training-data guesses | — | mcp-server-dev is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| model-effort-selection | technique | research | different phases need different reasoning depth | — | `/model`, `/effort`, `/fast`, or `/usage` in the transcript |
| official-plugins | technique | building-agents, building-mcp-integrations | ready to implement | Plugins installed from `claude-plugins-official` (visible via `/plugin list`) | `/plugin` usage; references marketplace plugins |
| plan-mode | technique | api-design, building-agents, building-mcp-integrations, code-understanding, debugging, dependency-management, devops, documentation, greenfield, incident-response, llm-features, migration, onboarding, performance, refactoring, release-management, research, tech-debt | starting a new API or major version from scratch | — | Enters plan mode (Shift+Tab); asks for a plan before edits |
| plugin-dev | plugin | building-skills-plugins | building a plugin and wanting scaffolding, validation, and review in one guided flow | — | plugin-dev is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |

| run | builtin-command | debugging, testing | you want to see the change working in the real app, not infer it from green tests | — | user ran /run in this conversation |
| safe-autonomy | technique | building-agents | need to constrain what the agent can do | `permissions` rules or `sandbox` configuration in settings | Discusses permission modes; runs with elevated autonomy deliberately |

| security-review | builtin-command | code-review, security | pending changes touch auth, input handling, or anything a CVE could grow from | — | user ran /security-review in this conversation |
| session-context-management | technique | code-understanding | long exploration is saturating the context window | — | `/context`, `/compact`, `/btw`, `/clear`, `/resume`, or `/branch` in the transcript |
| session-report | plugin | devops | token usage and cache efficiency need real numbers, not impressions | — | session-report is installed (its skills/commands are visible in the session) or its commands run in this conversation |
| simplify | builtin-command | refactoring, tech-debt | working code needs reuse, simplification, and efficiency cleanup — quality only, not bug hunting | — | user ran /simplify in this conversation |

| verify | builtin-command | code-review, testing | tests and typecheck pass but nobody has watched the change actually work end-to-end | — | user ran /verify in this conversation |
